urn:contentItem:6JHK-F5B3-RRV5-42XV-00000-00OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:27ZLexisNexisSTOCKOCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP

PermID5073147691Websitehttps://octopus.energy/IndustryRenewable Energy Supply & GenerationAddress"UK House, 5th floor, 164-182 Oxford Street London W1D 1NN England, United Kingdom"

ACTIVITIES:

Octopus Energy Group, founded in 2015 by Greg Jackson, is a renewable energy provider headquartered in London, UK. Employing over 3,500 people, it generates $5 billion annually, serving 7 million+ customers across 18 countries. Its Kraken platform optimizes energy usage. In 2024, Octopus expanded U.S. operations. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, innovation, and customer focus, with training in clean tech and AI. Competing with EDF, Octopus stands out for its flexible tariffs and smart grid tech. Recent initiatives include EV charging networks and net-zero commitments, aligning with green energy trends. Valued at $9 billion in 2023, Octopus drives the global energy transition.

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ WIND-WIN: OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION MAKES NEARLY EUR600 MILLION EUROPEAN WIND PUSH

321 MW of onshore wind added across 17 sites in France, Germany and Poland

Nearly EUR600m invested in Octopus' massive Europe-wide renewable push

These new wind farms will generate enough power for over 250,000 homes

London, 6th May 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's largest specialist renewables investors, is making a major Europe-wide push into the wind energy market, acquiring 321 MW onshore wind farms across 17 sites.

Its fund management team has invested EUR584 million in wind farms in France, Germany and Poland, on behalf of the Sky fund (ORI SCSp) it manages, accelerating homegrown power across key European markets. These wind farms will generate enough clean power for over 250,000 homes.

Source: Company Website

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP WIND-WIN: OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION MAKES NEARLY EUR600 MILLION EUROPEAN WIND PUSH

London, 6th May 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's largest specialist renewables investors, is making a major Europe-wide push into the wind energy market, acquiring 321 MW onshore wind farms across 17 sites.

Its fund management team has invested EUR584 million in wind farms in France, Germany and Poland, on behalf of the Sky fund (ORI SCSp) it manages, accelerating homegrown power across key European markets. These wind farms will generate enough clean power for over 250,000 homes.

Octopus now manages over 400 large-scale renewables projects across the globe, as it continues to scale cheap, green power at lightning speed. With these latest deals, it manages 67 onshore wind farms in Europe, including the UK, France, Germany, Poland, Ireland, Sweden and Finland.

Source: Company Website

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHM-3HK3-RX4C-P4JP-00000-00Veolia Environment Supports Community Pride Initiatives in Rockland2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:24ZLexisNexisVeolia Environment Supports Community Pride Initiatives in Rockland

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 10:01 EDT

Veolia Environment is actively involved in local community initiatives as Keep Rockland Beautiful (KRB) embarks on its 2026 Greatest American Cleanup campaign. The cleanup, which commenced on 1st April 2026 and will continue through 4th July 2026, seeks to engage residents in beautifying their county in alignment with the America 250 celebration. The initiative aims to attract a record number of volunteers to remove litter from streets, parks, and waterways, fostering a sense of community pride and environmental responsibility.

Marisa Marrone, Executive Director of Keep Rockland Beautiful, emphasized the importance of this year's campaign, stating, 'This year’s cleanup campaign is about more than just picking up litter; it’s about community pride, environmental responsibility, and coming together ahead of a historic celebration.' Veolia, known for its environmental stewardship, supports these efforts by encouraging local residents to participate in cleanup activities, which also provide opportunities for team-building and civic engagement.

The KRB initiative has been empowering residents for nearly 30 years through environmental education and sustainability initiatives. Volunteers can participate in organized cleanups or opt for independent cleanups, with KRB providing necessary supplies and coordination for trash removal. This flexibility allows individuals and groups to choose their preferred locations and dates, fostering a deeper understanding of environmental stewardship while enjoying the outdoors.

In addition to the cleanup campaign, Veolia's Watershed Recreation Program has officially opened for the 2026 season. This program offers residents in New York and New Jersey access to four scenic reservoirs for activities such as fishing, hiking, and birding. The program runs through 30th November 2026 and includes educational opportunities for children focused on conservation and environmental stewardship, further emphasizing Veolia's commitment to community engagement.

The overlap of these initiatives highlights the importance of local partnerships in fostering a healthy environment and community spirit. As the community prepares for the upcoming America 250 celebration, Veolia Environment’s support for the cleanup campaign and recreational programs plays a significant role in promoting environmental consciousness and community involvement in Rockland County.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 VEOLIA ENVIRONMENT ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Veolia Environment Activities

Activities

Veolia Environment is a global leader in environmental services, operating primarily in the water, waste, and energy sectors. The company provides comprehensive solutions for water treatment, distribution, and wastewater management; waste collection, recycling, and recovery; as well as energy efficiency services, including heating networks and renewable energy production. Its core activities support sustainable resource management, helping clients reduce environmental impact while optimizing operational costs. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, such as bioenergy and solar projects, and digital solutions for smart water and waste management, fueled by increasing demand for eco-friendly technologies. Veolia's innovative approach to the circular economy has strengthened its position in markets worldwide, contributing to global efforts in climate resilience and resource conservation. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 3rd largest Transportation company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Where is JPMorgan Chase ’s headquarters? Inside the banking giant’s new NYC tower

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK5-16P3-RTMW-V1W0-00000-00Where is JPMorgan Chase’s headquarters? Inside the banking giant’s new NYC tower2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:27ZLexisNexisWhere is JPMorgan Chase’s headquarters? Inside the banking giant’s new NYC tower

For over a century, skyscrapers have served as the ultimate symbols of corporate power and ambition, showcasing the latest in technological and engineering advancements while reaching ever higher toward the sky. Perhaps no other building better displays where a business has been — and where it’s ...

For over a century, skyscrapers have served as the ultimate symbols of corporate power and ambition, showcasing the latest in technological and engineering advancements while reaching ever higher toward the sky.

Perhaps no other building better displays where a business has been — and where it’s headed — than JPMorgan Chase’s ( JPM ) new headquarters, the supertall skyscraper at 270 Park Avenue.

A symphony of enduring materials such as recycled steel, glass, bronze, and copper, the building is notable as the largest “all-electric” skyscraper in New York, powered by 100% renewable energy .

It’s also one of the tallest buildings in the world, standing at 1,388 feet, or 60 stories high, which bestows it not just with skyscraper status but also inclusion in an elite group of “supertall” buildings that includes Hudson Yards and the Willis Tower in Chicago.  

Where is JPMorgan Chase’s headquarters?

JPMorgan Chasehas been in New York City since 1799 . Back then, it was known as the Manhattan Company, and its mission was to provide clean water to the city during an outbreak of yellow fever. However, businessman Aaron Burr (who later became Thomas Jefferson’s vice president) added a provision to the company’s charter that allowed it to use its surplus capital for banking, and thus a financial empire was born.

Today, JPMorgan Chase is the world’s largest bank by market cap , currently around $810 billion as of May 2026, and its new headquarters is located at the same address as its previous one — 270 Park Avenue. The company has called this location home since 1980; its former headquarters, the 52-story Union Carbide building , was demolished in 2019 to make way for the new structure.

Earlier still, between 1914 and 1980, JPMorgan Chase’s headquarters was located in the heart of the Financial District at 53 Wall Street . Housed in a distinctive neoclassical building at the corner of Wall and Broad Streets, The New York Post once called it a “temple to commerce.” That building is also known as the “House of Morgan.”

What are some unique features of JPMorgan Chase’s headquarters?

Technology takes center stage at JPMorgan Chase’s new headquarters, fueled by the company’s aggressive adoption of artificial intelligence — something CEO Jamie Dimon believes will impact “ virtually every function ” of its business.

270 Park Avenue “ reimagines the workplace ” for JPM’s employees because its digital footprint is as important as its physical infrastructure, with thousands of connected sensors and smart systems that make operations and collaboration both optimized and efficient.

For instance, workers can use a dedicated mobile app to navigate the building, book meeting rooms, and even order meals and coffee. HVAC systems deliver double the amount of fresh air, while advanced windows let in 30% more daylight, along with “circadian lighting” designed for wellness. Each floor is about twice the average height, making the 60-story tower more comparable in size to a 100-story building .

Related: How many employees does JPMorgan Chase have in 2026? Its workforce, locations, and layoffs explained

Security is, understandably, top of mind for a bank that manages $4.3 trillion in assets. And so, to gain access to the building, workers use biometric authentication, including palm and contactless retina scanning.

Dimon has called the 2.5-million-square-foot tower a “ beautiful physical manifestation ” of the company, while The Wall Street Journal dubbed it “ a monument to work ,” since it was built during Dimon's 5-day-a-week return to office (RTO) mandate.

Who designed JPM’s headquarters?

Foster + Partners served as the building’s architect — it also designed Apple Park in Cupertino, California.

Led by Norman Foster, this project drew inspiration from the grid system that defines New York City as well as “ the grandeur of the traditional banking hall .”

Related: Where is Apple’s headquarters? A spaceship-like office with thousands of trees

The tower’s eight floors are organized around a two-story atrium. There are multiple trading floors for the firm’s investment banking operations, along with boardrooms, collaborative work areas, executive offices, fitness centers, meditation rooms, and even a food hall designed by famed NYC restaurateur Danny Meyer.

Foster himself created the memorable art installation in the building’s lobby: an American flag that seems to perpetually wave from an “ unseen breeze .” 

Dow company histories:

History of Apple: Company timeline and facts History of Coca-Cola: Timeline, facts & milestones History of Nike: Company timeline and facts

270 Park Avenue cost a whopping $3 billion to construct, a sum Dimon justified by calling their new home " a headquarters for 50 years ."

Ten thousand JPM employees currently work at 270 Park Avenue, representing some of the firm's most important functions, including its investment banking, markets and trading, technology, and wealth management divisions, as well as its executive leadership team.

The project also added 8,000 new construction jobs and generated $2.6 billion worth of economic activity for New York City, according to JPM statements .

Can I tour JPMorgan Chase’s headquarters?

As the global headquarters of a private company, 270 Park Avenue is not open to the public.

However, due to its location just north of Grand Central Station, it is uniquely situated on top of the city’s underground rail lines, spurring some very creative design decisions. For example, the building is actually suspended 80 feet off the ground by a fan-shaped network of steel columns and bracing, which affords it twice as much outdoor public space as its previous building.

This is something JPM's designers took full advantage of when planning its street-level plaza, which is open and accessible to the public.

This space includes a garden and five large-scale installations by world-renowned artists, including Maya Lin, Leo Villareal, and Gerhard Richter.

Related: Where is Microsoft’s headquarters? Inside the tech giant’s corporate campus

value.Title: Fmr. Ogun commissioner, Adebajo joins FCMB Group

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ8-2B73-S7H1-G3RY-00000-00Fmr. Ogun commissioner, Adebajo joins FCMB Group2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisFmr. Ogun commissioner, Adebajo joins FCMB Group

A former commissioner for Agriculture in OgunAdepejuAdebajo has joined FCMB Group Plc as an Independent Non-Executive Director to its Board, following the approval of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN).

Adebajo who has served in various capacities is expected to bring her over 30 years of experience across industry, renewable energy, agriculture, finance, and consulting to the FCMB Group Board.

In a statement, it highlighted her specialisation in business transformation and turnarounds, with a strong record in strategy development, team leadership, and ESG implementation for clients in Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe.

It added that her background combines technical expertise and business insight, with degrees from Harvard Business School and Imperial College London, and further studies in Sustainability management.

'She has led teams in a range of sectors, including as CEO Cement at Lafarge Africa, UTC Nigeria, Mouka Limited and Lumos Nigeria, and AGM at UBA Plc. Earlier in her career, she worked with Boston Consulting Group,

supporting clients in London, Europe and the USA on business transformation and Citibank London.

'Adebajo also founded Pinky Blue Limited, the exclusive franchisee of Mothercare UK in Nigeria, and served as Commissioner for Agriculture in Ogun State from 2017 to 2019,' the statement reads.

It described her passion for climate and sustainability, saying she founded the Climate Governance Initiative Nigeria and served on the World Economic Forum's Global Future Council on Climate and Nature Governance.

'Her leadership has been recognised by Cranfield University, LinkedIn, CNBC Africa and Harvard Business School,' the statement added.

Adebajo has completed executive training at leading institutions, including the Advanced Management Programme at INSEAD, the Prince of Wales's Business and Sustainability Programme at the University of Cambridge, AI for Business Strategy at Massachusetts Institute of Technology Sloan School and the Citibank Management Trainee Programme in London and New York.

She currently serves as Chair of the Board of the Lagos State Employment Trust Fund and sits on the boards of Ibstock Plc, a UK FTSE 250 manufacturing company, Wolseley Limited, a UK private equity-owned distribution company and Husk Power Systems, a Distributed Energy Resources company providing power to communities in the Global South. She is Transformation Lead for Etex Group in Nigeria (Nigerite and Emenite).

She remains committed to increasing employment and employability, advancing the energy transition, sustainability, and mentoring women as an EMCC Qualified coach.

The Board of Directors welcomes Adebajo and believes her wealth of experience in business transformation, strategy development, operational excellence, and ESG implementation will strengthen FCMB Group's leadership and

support its continued growth in the Nigerian market.

Her appointment reflects the Group's ongoing commitment to maintaining a robust governance framework and building a leadership team that supports innovation, inclusivity, and responsible practices.

urn:contentItem:6JJ1-8F83-S1PF-M301-00000-00Fmr. Ogun commissioner, Adebajo joins FCMB Group2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisFmr. Ogun commissioner, Adebajo joins FCMB Group

A former commissioner for Agriculture in OgunAdepejuAdebajo has joined FCMB Group Plc as an Independent Non-Executive Director to its Board, following the approval of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN).

Adebajo who has served in various capacities is expected to bring her over 30 years of experience across industry, renewable energy, agriculture, finance, and consulting to the FCMB Group Board.

In a statement, it highlighted her specialisation in business transformation and turnarounds, with a strong record in strategy development, team leadership, and ESG implementation for clients in Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe.

It added that her background combines technical expertise and business insight, with degrees from Harvard Business School and Imperial College London, and further studies in Sustainability management.

'She has led teams in a range of sectors, including as CEO Cement at Lafarge Africa, UTC Nigeria, Mouka Limited and Lumos Nigeria, and AGM at UBA Plc. Earlier in her career, she worked with Boston Consulting Group,

supporting clients in London, Europe and the USA on business transformation and Citibank London.

'Adebajo also founded Pinky Blue Limited, the exclusive franchisee of Mothercare UK in Nigeria, and served as Commissioner for Agriculture in Ogun State from 2017 to 2019,' the statement reads.

It described her passion for climate and sustainability, saying she founded the Climate Governance Initiative Nigeria and served on the World Economic Forum's Global Future Council on Climate and Nature Governance.

'Her leadership has been recognised by Cranfield University, LinkedIn, CNBC Africa and Harvard Business School,' the statement added.

Adebajo has completed executive training at leading institutions, including the Advanced Management Programme at INSEAD, the Prince of Wales's Business and Sustainability Programme at the University of Cambridge, AI for Business Strategy at Massachusetts Institute of Technology Sloan School and the Citibank Management Trainee Programme in London and New York.

She currently serves as Chair of the Board of the Lagos State Employment Trust Fund and sits on the boards of Ibstock Plc, a UK FTSE 250 manufacturing company, Wolseley Limited, a UK private equity-owned distribution company and Husk Power Systems, a Distributed Energy Resources company providing power to communities in the Global South. She is Transformation Lead for Etex Group in Nigeria (Nigerite and Emenite).

She remains committed to increasing employment and employability, advancing the energy transition, sustainability, and mentoring women as an EMCC Qualified coach.

The Board of Directors welcomes Adebajo and believes her wealth of experience in business transformation, strategy development, operational excellence, and ESG implementation will strengthen FCMB Group's leadership and

support its continued growth in the Nigerian market.

Her appointment reflects the Group's ongoing commitment to maintaining a robust governance framework and building a leadership team that supports innovation, inclusivity, and responsible practices.

value.Title: The Caribbean Is Facing a Critical Turning Point as Economic and Political Pressures Grow

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-7W83-SFTP-Y404-00000-00The Caribbean Is Facing a Critical Turning Point as Economic and Political Pressures Grow2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:27ZLexisNexisNEWSThe Caribbean Is Facing a Critical Turning Point as Economic and Political Pressures GrowIngrid Jones

Image credit: SueSchweiz

The Caribbean is entering one of the most important periods in its modern history as governments across the region attempt to balance economic survival, climate resilience and political independence in an increasingly unstable world. While tourism continues driving much of the economy, there is a growing realization that relying too heavily on visitors, cruise traffic and foreign spending leaves island nations vulnerable whenever the global economy slows down.

That reality became painfully obvious during the pandemic years when airports shut down, resorts emptied and entire economies suddenly stalled almost overnight. Even though tourism has largely recovered, many leaders are now pushing to diversify into industries like renewable energy, agriculture, digital services, offshore finance and shipping logistics. The goal is not to abandon tourism, but to reduce the risk of having entire national economies tied almost entirely to one sector.

Inflation is also creating serious pressure for ordinary families throughout the region. Food prices, fuel costs and imported goods have climbed steadily over the past few years, and many countries remain heavily dependent on imports for basic necessities. In several island nations, wages have struggled to keep up with the rising cost of living, creating frustration among younger generations who increasingly feel squeezed between low wages and limited opportunities.

Climate change remains another major concern. Stronger storms, coastal erosion and rising sea levels are no longer future threats being discussed only in scientific reports. They are visible realities affecting infrastructure, insurance costs and long-term development planning today. Some governments are spending enormous portions of their budgets simply rebuilding roads, ports and public infrastructure after severe weather events.

Crime and security have also become growing political issues in parts of the region. Gang violence, illegal firearms and narcotics trafficking continue placing pressure on law enforcement agencies that often operate with limited resources. Several governments are now trying to combine tougher policing with expanded social programs aimed at reducing youth unemployment and economic desperation.

At the geopolitical level, global powers are paying far closer attention to the Caribbean than many people realize. China has increased infrastructure investments and diplomatic outreach across several island states, while the United States continues viewing the region as strategically important due to its proximity and shipping routes. European nations also maintain deep economic and historical ties through trade, tourism and development partnerships. Smaller countries are increasingly trying to balance those competing relationships carefully without becoming too dependent on any single foreign power.

Despite the economic and political challenges, the Caribbean continues to have one of the strongest cultural influences anywhere in the world relative to its size. Music genres born in the region continue shaping global entertainment, while Caribbean communities abroad have had a profound impact on cities like Toronto, London, Miami and New York. From reggae and soca to cuisine, athletics and fashion, the influence extends far beyond the islands themselves.

There is also a generational shift beginning to emerge. Younger entrepreneurs and political voices are talking more openly about economic modernization, food security, regional cooperation and keeping skilled talent from permanently leaving for opportunities overseas. Many believe the future of the region depends on building stronger local industries capable of supporting younger populations long term.

The challenges remain significant, but so does the resilience. The Caribbean has spent generations adapting to economic hardship, natural disasters and outside political pressures while still maintaining a strong cultural identity and sense of community. That resilience is one of the reasons many leaders remain cautiously optimistic despite the growing uncertainty facing the global economy.

What happens over the next decade could shape the future of the region for generations. The countries that successfully modernize their economies while protecting their culture and independence may emerge far stronger than many outsiders currently expect.

urn:contentItem:6JK3-2XJ3-S207-D23B-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:27ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREA INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S NEW ETF CAPTIVATES INVESTORS WITH 100 BILLION WON MILESTONE

The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF, managed by Korea Investment Management Co., Ltd., has captivated the market by surpassing 100 billion won in net assets in less than a week since its listing. As of 22nd April 2026, the net asset value of the ETF, which debuted on the Korea Exchange on 14th April 2026, reached 103.7 billion won, representing a staggering ninefold increase from its initial 11 billion won on the first trading day.Unlike traditional ETFs that focus solely on aerospace and defense sectors, Korea Investment Management's active ETF is strategically concentrated on 'NewSpace' services. This includes companies engaged in reusable launch vehicles, artificial intelligence-based satellite data analytics, and space data centers. Notable holdings include EchoStar (22.65%), Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, and MDA Space. EchoStar, which has a stake in SpaceX, holds the highest weighting among all ETFs listed in Korea, while MDA Space and York Space Systems are exclusive to this ETF.The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF has demonstrated impressive performance since its launch, delivering a return of 7.63%, the highest among the nine space and aerospace ETFs currently available in Korea. This strong performance has attracted significant interest from individual investors, who have cumulatively net purchased 52.6 billion won over the six trading days following the ETF's introduction.Kim Hyuntae, Head of Global Quantitative Investment at Korea Investment Management and the fund's portfolio manager, emphasized the importance of an agile approach to managing the ETF. He stated, 'Given that the space industry is still in the early stages of growth, we determined that it is important to construct a portfolio focused on high-growth, pure space technology companies and to respond quickly to market changes, so we launched the product as an active ETF.' He further noted that the active management allows for swift incorporation of new listings, such as SpaceX, directly responding to market events.As the space industry continues to evolve, Korea Investment Management's ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The rapid accumulation of assets within a short timeframe underscores the growing investor confidence in this innovative approach to space technology investments.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION SPOTLIGHTS MID-MARKET OPPORTUNITIES AT NEW YORK FORUM

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) hosted the 28th 'New York Finance Forum' at the Korea Society in New York, focusing on the burgeoning mid-market segment in North American infrastructure investments. The event took place on the 16th of April 2026, drawing over 40 participants from government, public investment institutions, and various financial sectors.During the forum, Northleaf Capital Partners presented key insights into the mid-market infrastructure investment landscape, highlighting its structural advantages. This segment typically includes deals involving companies valued at approximately USD 1 billion or less, though the threshold has recently expanded to include transactions up to USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics and asset manager criteria.Jared Waldron, Co-Head of Infrastructure at Northleaf, underscored that despite 86% of North American infrastructure investment transactions occurring within the mid-market, institutional funds remain heavily concentrated in larger funds. This disparity creates a less competitive environment, allowing investors to secure more favorable investment opportunities.Jessica Kennedy, Managing Director at Northleaf, further elaborated on the advantages of mid-market infrastructure investments, noting that they offer opportunities for differentiated results through active value creation and multiple exit strategies. Investors can thus anticipate attractive risk-adjusted returns from this segment.Yuryoung Kim, Head of KIC's New York office and organizer of the forum, expressed optimism that the discussions would assist domestic institutional investors in refining their overseas alternative investment strategies. KIC continues to operate in major global financial hubs, including New York, San Francisco, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, facilitating the exchange of investment information and fostering collaborative networks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.

APPENDIX II STOCK IDENTIFIERS

SECTION 1 CORPORATE PROFILE

1.1 Activities

Oklo Inc. is a pioneering company in the nuclear energy sector, specializing in the development and deployment of advanced fission reactors to deliver safe, carbon-free power. Headquartered in the United States and listed on the New York Stock Exchange, the company focuses on innovative technologies such as fast reactors and fuel recycling, which enhance energy efficiency and minimize nuclear waste. Its fastest-growing segments include the design and commercialization of small modular reactors (SMRs), which are gaining momentum due to their compact size, scalability, and ability to integrate with renewable energy systems. Oklo is also expanding through strategic partnerships with utilities and government entities, accelerating regulatory approvals and deployment efforts. This positions the company as a key contributor to the global shift toward sustainable energy, addressing rising demands for reliable, low-emission power solutions in data centers, industrial operations, and beyond.

Encompass Health Corporation (NYSE:EHC) is driving significant growth through strategic initiatives, as exemplified by the Encompass Health Deaconess Rehabilitation Hospital Newburgh. The Encompass Health Newburgh expansion is part of the Deaconess Rehabilitation Hospital expansion Newburgh project that enhances the Newburgh inpatient rehabilitation hospital's capabilities. A key focus is on Encompass Health Deaconess Newburgh services, addressing Newburgh rehabilitation hospital expansion needs. This includes inpatient rehab NewburghNY and post-acute care Newburgh, alongside physical therapy Newburgh Encompass Health offerings. The facility provides occupational therapy Newburgh and speech therapy Newburgh, specializing in stroke rehabilitation Newburgh and orthopedic rehabilitation Newburgh, with comprehensive neurological rehabilitation Newburgh programs. The Encompass Health expansion New York effort supports new Encompass Health Deaconess jobs Newburgh and Encompass Health careers Newburgh. The expansion staged by an Encompass Health press release Newburgh aligns with the Encompass Health EHC expansion news impacting EHC stock Encompass Health news. Encompass Health home health Newburgh, patient rehabilitation services Newburgh, inpatient rehab center Newburgh, and post-surgical rehab Newburgh are core elements of the Encompass Health hospital expansion Hudson Valley, reinforcing its rehabilitation facility near NewburghNY. Through Encompass Health community outreach Newburgh and the Encompass Health hospital beds expansion Newburgh, new rehab services Newburgh are introduced, supporting rehab hospital jobs Newburgh and enhancing Encompass Health's leadership in post-acute care markets.

SECTION 2 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES, TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

2.1 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 101 words

Aurora-INL Groundbreaking: Construction Begins on Flagship 75-MWe Fast Reactor

Oklo has commenced physical construction of its 75-MWe Aurora fast reactor at Idaho National Laboratory (INL). This milestone, supported by a 2019 site use permit and DOE authorization, transitions the project from design to development. Kiewit Nuclear Solutions will lead construction. The project utilizes metallic HALEU fuel derived from the historic EBR-II reactor, with fuel fabrication occurring at INL. While originally slated for a 2027-2028 launch, participation in the DOE's Reactor Pilot Program could accelerate criticality to a July 2026 target. This project represents a significant scale-up from a previously denied 1.5-MW prototype, demonstrating technological maturation and a modernized regulatory strategy.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 98 words

Tennessee Advanced Fuel Center Announcement: $1.7 Billion Investment in Oak Ridge Fuel Recycling Facility

Oklo announced a $1.68 billion investment to build an advanced nuclear fuel center in Oak Ridge, Tennessee, including the first U.S. privately funded commercial fuel recycling facility. Set to begin production in the early 2030s, the center will produce metallic HALEU fuel for Aurora reactors by recycling used fuel from conventional reactors. The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) has expressed interest in a partnership, potentially creating a closed-loop fuel cycle. This initiative aligns with recent policy shifts away from the 1977 moratorium on reprocessing and is crucial for establishing the fuel supply infrastructure for Oklo's planned 14-gigawatt reactor fleet.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

Siemens Energy Binding Contract: Power Conversion System for Aurora Powerhouse

Oklo has executed a binding contract with Siemens Energy for the design and delivery of the power conversion system for the Aurora-INL reactor. The agreement covers an SST-600 steam turbine and an SGen-100A generator, which will interface with a supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle. This move de-risks the project by securing long-lead components from an established global manufacturer. The design leverages proven, commercially available turbomachinery to reduce technical risk and shorten timelines. The contract is a key step in solidifying the supply chain for Oklo's broader 14-gigawatt commercial pipeline.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 92 words

DOE Nuclear Reactor Pilot Program Selection: Three Projects Fast-Tracked for Aurora and Atomic Alchemy

The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) selected Oklo for three projects in its new Reactor Pilot Program: the Aurora-INL reactor, Atomic Alchemy's VIPR radioisotope reactor, and a third project for fuel development. This provides an accelerated regulatory pathway under DOE authorization, with a target for criticality by July 2026. Oklo's subsidiary, Atomic Alchemy, was also selected for the Advanced Nuclear Fuel Line Pilot Program. Oklo's unique position, including existing permits, allocated fuel, and advanced designs, has allowed it to progress faster than competitors within the program, validating its technology and regulatory strategy.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 91 words

NRC Pre-Application Readiness Assessment Completion: Regulatory Pathway Advancement

Oklo successfully completed the NRC's pre-application readiness assessment for Phase 1 of its combined license application (COLA) for the Aurora-INL project. The NRC affirmed Oklo's readiness to submit its formal application for siting and environmental aspects, identifying no significant regulatory gaps. This proactive engagement, building on years of interaction with the NRC, is expected to streamline the formal review process. Supported by the ADVANCE Act and reduced NRC fees, this milestone validates Oklo's licensing strategy and enhances investor confidence as the company prepares to submit the formal COLA later in 2025.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 82 words

newcleo Strategic Partnership with $2 Billion Transatlantic Investment Commitment

Oklo has formed a strategic partnership with European developer newcleo, securing a commitment of up to $2 billion in investment for U.S. fuel fabrication and manufacturing. The collaboration aims to advance a closed-loop fuel cycle and explore the disposition of surplus plutonium as a fuel source, transforming a liability into a valuable asset. This transatlantic cooperation enhances energy security and validates Oklo's vertically integrated fuel strategy. The agreement also builds upon previous collaborations and signals strong international confidence in Oklo's commercialization plans.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 85 words

Atomic Alchemy Acquisition Closure: Radioisotope Producer Strategic Addition

Oklo completed its acquisition of radioisotope producer Atomic Alchemy for $25 million in an all-stock transaction. Atomic Alchemy, now a subsidiary, is developing the VIPR reactor to produce medical and industrial radioisotopes, a market with high demand and constrained supply. This strategic move diversifies Oklo's revenue streams beyond electricity generation, secures a position in the domestic radioisotope supply chain, and leverages synergies in regulatory expertise and materials handling. The acquisition also positions Atomic Alchemy to benefit from accelerated authorization through the DOE Reactor Pilot Program.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP) Memorandum of Understanding for Global Aurora Deployment

Oklo signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power (KHNP) to support the standard design and global deployment of the Aurora Powerhouse. KHNP, a world-leading nuclear builder, will contribute extensive expertise in reactor construction, supply chain development, and operational management. The partnership is critical for scaling up manufacturing and construction to meet Oklo's 14-gigawatt commercial pipeline commitments. The collaboration leverages KHNP's project delivery experience to accelerate Oklo's path to commercialization and sets the stage for future international market entry.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 81 words

$400 Million Equity Offering and $1.5 Billion At-The-Market Program: Capital Market Access and Financing Strategy

Oklo has significantly strengthened its financial position through a $400 million public equity offering and the establishment of a $1.5 billion at-the-market (ATM) program. These moves provide substantial capital and flexible access to funding for project development, manufacturing, and strategic investments. As of November 2025, the company's liquidity stood at approximately $2.7 billion, providing a multi-year runway that far exceeds its projected cash burn. This demonstrated market confidence eliminates near-term refinancing pressures and positions Oklo to execute its 14-gigawatt commercial pipeline.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 95 words

DOE Aurora Fuel Fabrication Facility (A3F) Nuclear Safety Design Agreement Approval: Accelerated Authorization Pathway

The DOE approved the Nuclear Safety Design Agreement (NSDA) for Oklo's Aurora Fuel Fabrication Facility (A3F) at Idaho National Laboratory. This approval, granted in just two weeks, authorizes the fabrication of metallic HALEU fuel for the Aurora-INL reactor and sets a precedent for the DOE's new accelerated fuel production framework. Oklo also expanded its collaboration with INL's operator to use the Aurora reactor for advanced fuel and materials research, integrating commercial operations with R&D. This milestone enables the transition from fuel design to production, a critical step in building a domestic advanced fuel supply chain.

2.2 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 278 words

Apr 27, 2026: X-Energy IPO Sets New Capital Markets Benchmark for Advanced Nuclear, Increasing Strategic Pressure on Oklo

Amazon-backed advanced nuclear reactor developer X-Energy priced its upsized initial public offering on April 23, 2026, raising approximately $1.02 billion by selling 44.3 million Class A shares at $23 each, well above the marketed range of $16 to $19, according to industry reports and the company's official announcement. Shares began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker "XE" on April 24, 2026.

The offering is anchored by significant commercial commitments. Amazon holds a binding agreement to procure up to 5 gigawatts of nuclear power by 2039, while Dow Inc. has contracted to source industrial process heat and power from an Xe-100 deployment at its Seadrift, Texas chemical complex.

The successful pricing comes amid intensifying electricity demand from hyperscale AI data centers and industrial reshoring, signaling that institutional capital markets now view small modular reactor technology as commercially fundable infrastructure.

Impact on Oklo Inc.: OKLO

Following this news, Oklo Inc. faces a recalibrated competitive landscape in advanced nuclear, where X-Energy's $1.02 billion raise establishes a fresh public-market reference point. Oklo, which uses a fast-spectrum sodium-cooled Aurora powerhouse design targeting smaller-scale baseload applications, now competes for hyperscaler and industrial customer contracts against an X-Energy that is significantly better-capitalized for fuel manufacturing and reactor deployment.

Both companies target similar customer archetypes, including data center operators, remote industrial sites, and utilities seeking 24/7 carbon-free power. Oklo's strategic differentiation lies in its build-own-operate model, where it sells power rather than reactors, contrasting with X-Energy's reactor-and-fuel sales approach. The IPO pressures Oklo to demonstrate accelerated NRC licensing progress and customer offtake commitments comparable to X-Energy's anchors.

ECO NEWS 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 314 words

Apr 24, 2026: X-energy's $1.02 Billion Nasdaq Debut Introduces a Better-Capitalized Publicly Listed Rival to Oklo in the Small Modular Reactor Market

X-energy, Inc., a Rockville, Maryland-based developer of small modular reactor (SMR) technology and proprietary TRISO fuel, priced its upsized initial public offering at $23.00 per share on April 23, 2026. The company sold 44,254,659 shares of Class A common stock, raising approximately $1.02 billion in gross proceeds before underwriting discounts. Pricing came $4 above the top of the marketed $16.00-to-$19.00 range, and the deal was upsized from the 42.86 million shares originally filed. Underwriters received a 30-day option to purchase up to 6,638,198 additional shares.

Shares begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on April 24, 2026 under the ticker symbol "XE," with the offering expected to close on April 27, 2026. J.P. Morgan, Morgan Stanley, Jefferies, and Moelis & Company served as lead joint book-running managers.

The listing arrives as investor interest in advanced nuclear technology has accelerated amid rising electricity demand from data centers and industrial electrification, sharpening competitive dynamics across the advanced-reactor value chain.

Impact on Oklo: OKLO

Following this development, Oklo faces a publicly listed peer with over $1 billion of fresh equity capital competing for the same utility and hyperscale data-center customers Oklo targets with its Aurora powerhouse. While Oklo's fast-reactor design and build-own-operate model remain differentiated, X-energy's Xe-100 platform and integrated TRISO-X fuel franchise bring a competing value proposition, with extension into industrial process heat, to public markets at scale. The above-range pricing sets a higher capital-raising benchmark for future Oklo funding rounds and follow-on offerings.

Oklo will now compete for NRC licensing bandwidth, nuclear engineering talent, and Department of Energy cost-share awards against a rival pursuing parallel deployment milestones with Dow and Energy Northwest. Category validation is directionally favorable for Oklo's narrative, though the narrowed capital-access gap tightens competitive dynamics across commercial negotiations and customer qualification processes.

urn:contentItem:6JK2-7YM3-RS4R-2101-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:27ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREA INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S NEW ETF CAPTIVATES INVESTORS WITH 100 BILLION WON MILESTONE

The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF, managed by Korea Investment Management Co., Ltd., has captivated the market by surpassing 100 billion won in net assets in less than a week since its listing. As of 22nd April 2026, the net asset value of the ETF, which debuted on the Korea Exchange on 14th April 2026, reached 103.7 billion won, representing a staggering ninefold increase from its initial 11 billion won on the first trading day.Unlike traditional ETFs that focus solely on aerospace and defense sectors, Korea Investment Management's active ETF is strategically concentrated on 'NewSpace' services. This includes companies engaged in reusable launch vehicles, artificial intelligence-based satellite data analytics, and space data centers. Notable holdings include EchoStar (22.65%), Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, and MDA Space. EchoStar, which has a stake in SpaceX, holds the highest weighting among all ETFs listed in Korea, while MDA Space and York Space Systems are exclusive to this ETF.The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF has demonstrated impressive performance since its launch, delivering a return of 7.63%, the highest among the nine space and aerospace ETFs currently available in Korea. This strong performance has attracted significant interest from individual investors, who have cumulatively net purchased 52.6 billion won over the six trading days following the ETF's introduction.Kim Hyuntae, Head of Global Quantitative Investment at Korea Investment Management and the fund's portfolio manager, emphasized the importance of an agile approach to managing the ETF. He stated, 'Given that the space industry is still in the early stages of growth, we determined that it is important to construct a portfolio focused on high-growth, pure space technology companies and to respond quickly to market changes, so we launched the product as an active ETF.' He further noted that the active management allows for swift incorporation of new listings, such as SpaceX, directly responding to market events.As the space industry continues to evolve, Korea Investment Management's ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The rapid accumulation of assets within a short timeframe underscores the growing investor confidence in this innovative approach to space technology investments.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION SPOTLIGHTS MID-MARKET OPPORTUNITIES AT NEW YORK FORUM

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) hosted the 28th 'New York Finance Forum' at the Korea Society in New York, focusing on the burgeoning mid-market segment in North American infrastructure investments. The event took place on the 16th of April 2026, drawing over 40 participants from government, public investment institutions, and various financial sectors.During the forum, Northleaf Capital Partners presented key insights into the mid-market infrastructure investment landscape, highlighting its structural advantages. This segment typically includes deals involving companies valued at approximately USD 1 billion or less, though the threshold has recently expanded to include transactions up to USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics and asset manager criteria.Jared Waldron, Co-Head of Infrastructure at Northleaf, underscored that despite 86% of North American infrastructure investment transactions occurring within the mid-market, institutional funds remain heavily concentrated in larger funds. This disparity creates a less competitive environment, allowing investors to secure more favorable investment opportunities.Jessica Kennedy, Managing Director at Northleaf, further elaborated on the advantages of mid-market infrastructure investments, noting that they offer opportunities for differentiated results through active value creation and multiple exit strategies. Investors can thus anticipate attractive risk-adjusted returns from this segment.Yuryoung Kim, Head of KIC's New York office and organizer of the forum, expressed optimism that the discussions would assist domestic institutional investors in refining their overseas alternative investment strategies. KIC continues to operate in major global financial hubs, including New York, San Francisco, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, facilitating the exchange of investment information and fostering collaborative networks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.
