urn:contentItem:6JJY-2WG3-RXKX-V10J-00000-00DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - THE RECENT NEWS OF DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:27ZLexisNexisSTOCKDAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - THE RECENT NEWS OF DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP

PermID4296380340Websitehttp://www.davispolk.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress450 Lexington Avenue New York, NY 10017 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, founded in 1849, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 1,000 lawyers across 10 offices, it serves clients in finance, technology, and healthcare, specializing in M&A, capital markets, and regulatory matters. In 2020, it advised on Morgan Stanleys $13 billion E*Trade acquisition and Gilead Sciences $21 billion Immunomedics deal. In 2024, it expanded its Menlo Park office with key hires like Paul Scrivano from Ropes & Gray. Generating over $1.5 billion annually, its culture emphasizes collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with Cravath, Davis Polk stands out for its dealmaking prowess, ranking second in 2020 M&A league tables. Recent initiatives include AI-driven legal tech and ESG advisory services.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY TECHNOLOGY RAISES HK$4.4 BILLION IN HONG KONG LISTING BACKED BY MAJOR LAW FIRMS

Sigenergy Technology, a Shanghai-based maker of energy storage systems, has closed its Hong Kong initial public offering, raising HK$4.4 billion, roughly $562 million. The deal was steered by Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, which provided critical legal guidance throughout the listing and after-market processes. The listing was recorded on the 17th of April 2026. Launched in May 2022, Sigenergy focuses on distributed energy storage systems and provides stackable, solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions. The company commands a 28.6 percent share in the global market for stackable all-in-one DESS products, illustrating its position in a sector expected to expand as demand for clean energy storage grows. The IPO was underwritten by a consortium led by CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation acting as joint sponsor. Underwriters were supported by Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ Christine Xu overseeing Greater China ECM and capital markets partner Lipton Li, alongside counsel Queenie Tong. In a broader deal flow for the region, Mindspace Business Parks REIT agreed to acquire the International Tech Park in Chennai for 30 billion rupees ($321 million), advised by Khaitan & Co. Separately, Indian asset manager SBI Funds Management is preparing for a 130-billion-rupee IPO, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising. The Sigenergy IPO underscores growing investor appetite for renewable energy and storage technologies.

+ DAVIS POLK-FOUNDED LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL CLOSES SIXTH FUND AT $2.5B

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has secured $2.5 billion for its sixth fund, signaling continued investor appetite for the manager's approach. The announcement was made public on April 14, 2026.The capital will be deployed in the financial services and technology sectors, areas the firm believes offer resilience and upside in the current market.Lightyear plans to back both established companies and innovative startups, leveraging its network and operating capabilities to help portfolio companies scale.Davis Polk & Wardwell has played a hands-on role, with its attorneys aiding in fund structuring and regulatory navigation, strengthening Lightyear's execution capabilities.Looking ahead, investors will be watching how Lightyear deploys the capital and whether it sustains strong returns amid macro and sector-specific challenges.

+ DAVIS POLK BETS ON LOS ANGELES GROWTH WITH NEW LA OFFICE

Davis Polk & Wardwell announced on 14th April 2026 that it plans to open a Los Angeles office and appoint Jason Russell to lead the expansion.Russell, who previously led Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom's Los Angeles office and served on its policy committee, brings a track record of handling high-profile matters for clients including the NBA, Christie’s and MGA Entertainment.The firm said the hire will help establish a sustained presence in California and is expected to attract additional talent as the new office is staffed.The Los Angeles project reflects Davis Polk's strategy to broaden its geographic footprint and compete with other major California firms for marquee matters.As the opening progresses, observers will watch how Russell leverages his relationships to grow the firm's practice in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LONDON GROWTH PUSHES DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON TO A NEW REVENUE MILESTONE

Debevoise & Plimpton disclosed a 40% revenue jump in its London office to $268 million, announced on 7th April 2026.The growth is underpinned by stronger demand across corporate law and dispute resolution as clients seek comprehensive legal solutions.The firm has been selective in high-value engagements and has adapted to market shifts.Industry observers say the performance signals momentum, with plans to invest in talent and broaden service lines.As the legal landscape evolves, competition remains intense, making sustained financial momentum a priority for the firm.

+ SECOND CIRCUIT AFFIRMS YPF VICTORY, DISMISSING MORE THAN $16 BILLION IN CLAIMS

The Second Circuit upheld the district court's finding that YPF had no contractual obligations to Petersen Energia Inversora S.A.U., Petersen Energia S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management L.P., together with its associated funds. The ruling is a clear victory for YPF and its defense team against the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.

+ DEBEVOISE APPOINTS SULLIVAN KC AND POPOVA TO HEAD EUROPEAN DISPUTES

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has named Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately, signaling a deeper push into Paris, Frankfurt and Luxembourg. The announcement was made on the 17th of March, 2026 and reflects the firm's strategy to strengthen international dispute resolution capabilities across mainland Europe.The appointment establishes regional leadership intended to drive Debevoise's dispute resolution practice in Europe alongside its other regional leaders in Latin America and Asia.Catherine Amirfar, Co-Chair of the firm's global International Dispute Resolution Group, said that Sullivan and Popova will build on Debevoise's long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe and will continue delivering top-tier advocacy and strategic counsel to clients.Sullivan KC is recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law, and in the new role he will focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations for a diverse client base that includes companies and private equity sponsors; he also has experience as an arbitrator in significant cases.Popova brings extensive experience in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on managing and litigating complex disputes; her dual civil and common-law qualifications, along with fluency in multiple languages, position her to navigate cross-border disputes, and she previously served on the ICCInternational Court of Arbitration.With more than four decades of experience, Debevoise’s International Dispute Resolution Group has delivered outcomes across 125+ countries and matters over $110 billion, underscoring the firm's commitment to achieving significant results for clients.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ COHEN RECOUNTS HALF-CENTURY OF BANKING REGULATION AND CRISIS WORK

H. Rodgin "Rodge" Cohen, the longtime senior chair of Sullivan & Cromwell, has been at the center of banking regulation for more than five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law’s Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen spoke with candor about the arc of the modern financial system, with Jay Clayton, former SEC chairman, and Edward Rock, ICGF co-director, providing additional perspective.Cohen traced regulatory evolution from the postwar era to today, emphasizing how landmark measures reshaped access to banking. He and Clayton highlighted the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act as turning points that broadened inclusion and redefined compliance. Clayton noted the shifts since the 1970s, while Cohen underscored how these changes altered banks’ operations and risk profiles.A defining chapter in Cohen’s career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, when he represented several major banks as policymakers moved to stabilize the system. He recalled the critical hours before the government’s intervention after Lehman Brothers’ collapse, saying, "We were on the edge of a cliff," underscoring the urgency of decisive action and solidifying his reputation as a leading financial-services lawyer. He also recalled the interstate banking expansion of the 1980s, which reshaped the banking landscape, with Clayton praising Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively.As they looked to the future, Cohen warned that cryptocurrency regulation must keep pace with rapid market evolution. He cautioned that "bad regulation drives out good regulation" and urged robust frameworks to manage crypto risk, noting that such governance will continue to shape the sector and the role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL EXPANDS LONDON TEAM WITH TWO PARTNERS FOCUSED ON PRIVATE CAPITAL

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP has added Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh as partners in its London office, a move that strengthens the firm’s private capital capabilities in the UK and Europe. The hires were announced with the firm’s expansion plans dated Jan. 21, 2026. McLaughlin will oversee London’s private equity sponsor and borrower acquisitions as well as leveraged finance, while McVeigh will lead the European private credit offering, concentrating on direct and specialty lending.Co-Chairs Robert Giuffra and Scott Miller expressed enthusiasm for the hires, noting that their top-tier expertise will enhance the firm’s service to private equity sponsors, lenders, and private credit providers across the region.McLaughlin has a recognized track record in acquisition and leveraged finance law in Europe, with accolades including Leading Individual for Leveraged Finance from Chambers UK and Leading Partner for Acquisition Finance from Legal 500. McVeigh is acclaimed for leveraged finance work in private credit and specialty lending, positioning both lawyers to bolster Sullivan & Cromwell’s private capital practice.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB [3 News That Matters]

+ JENNIFER MORTON JOINS CLEARY GOTTLIEB AS PARTNER IN THE FIRM'S FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS PRACTICE

Cleary Gottlieb has announced the addition of Jennifer Morton as a partner in its New York office, enhancing its award-winning financial institutions practice. This development was confirmed on the 7th of May 2026, marking a significant step in the firm's ongoing efforts to bolster its capabilities in financial services regulation.Morton brings more than 20 years of experience in broker-dealer regulation and transactions, with an advisory practice that covers complex regulatory and transactional matters, including public and private mergers and acquisitions and SEC-registered offerings, as well as the evolving landscape of digital assets and blockchain technology.Morton's background is complemented by strong leadership within the firm. Managing Partner Jeff Karpf said Morton is a great addition who will deepen the firm’s ability to deliver comprehensive financial services regulatory advice globally. Hugh Conroy, Jr., who heads the financial institutions practice, noted that her insights will be valuable for U.S. market entry and regulatory compliance.Morton has been recognized as a leading financial services lawyer by Chambers USA and in 2024 received the Women in Business Law Financial Regulation Lawyer of the Year Award from the International Financial Law Review, underscoring her contributions to the field.This hire follows Cleary Gottlieb’s recent expansion in the financial institutions group, including the April 20 addition of Igor Kleyman, who focuses on digital assets and fintech, and prior senior capital markets partners who joined earlier this year, reinforcing the firm’s leadership in financial services.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB EXTENDS LEAD AMONG BANKS AND MAJOR LATIN AMERICAN CORPORATES

Cleary Gottlieb has again topped LACCA's ranking as the most popular international firm advising Latin America's 100 largest banks, with 37 bank clients as of 6th May 2026. In addition, the firm is highlighted as a leading international adviser for Latin America's Top 100 companies by revenue, representing 51 of the region's biggest corporates.The dual recognition highlights Cleary Gottlieb's sustained excellence in both financial-services work and cross-border corporate matters. Industry observers point to the firm's deep local expertise, global network and longstanding relationships with major financial institutions as drivers of its market leadership. LACCA notes that findings are available to subscribers, underscoring the breadth of Cleary Gottlieb's Latin American practice.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB ADDS NEW PARTNER TO LONDON GLOBAL FUNDS PRACTICE

Cleary Gottlieb announced that David Christmas will join the London office as a partner in its Global Funds practice, effective May 5th, 2026. The hire marks the firm's seventh partner hire this year as it continues to expand its private capital capabilities in a competitive market.Christmas brings broad experience in fund formation across alternative asset classes and has a notable background in GP-led secondary transactions, positioning him to contribute across Cleary Gottlieb's global platforms. London partner Tihir Sarkar, a member of the Executive Committee, said that expanding the firm's global funds and private capital capabilities remains a principal strategic priority and noted that the London office has doubled its partner headcount since 2021.The firm notes that its Global Funds team has been recognized as Band 1 for Fund Formation in the United States by Chambers and has advised on funds with commitments exceeding $500 billion over its 30-year history.Managing Partner Jeff Karpf highlighted the sophistication of the transactions the team handles and said the addition of Christmas will strengthen the funds platform across transatlantic, Asian, and bicoastal U.S. coverage.Christmas is recognized for private equity work, including a selection by Law.com International in 2022 as a Private Equity Rising Star and as a Leading Partner in Investment Funds by the Legal 500, underscoring his credentials. The London office has been on a growth trajectory, following a record year in 2025 that saw 41 partners promoted or hired globally across practices including corporate, funds, and litigation.As Cleary Gottlieb continues to attract top talent, the firm aims to meet evolving needs of asset management clients diversifying across private equity, credit, real assets, and secondaries, with Christmas joining a team positioned to extend its leadership in the market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ PAREX RESOURCES SECURES $500 MILLION NOTE FINANCING WITH LATHAM & WATKINS COUNSEL

Latham & Watkins advised on the private placement of $500 million in 8.50% five-year notes by Parex Resources Inc., with the transaction officially recorded on 11th May 2026. The proceeds are earmarked for the acquisition of Frontera Energy Corporation's Colombian upstream business, which encompasses exploration and production facilities, a reverse osmosis water treatment facility, and a palm oil plantation.Frontera Energy Corporation, a Canadian public company, operates across South America in oil and natural gas exploration, development, production, transportation, storage, and sale.Latham's corporate deal team, led by David Miller, Roderick Branch, and Sam Rettew, with counsel Manasi Bhattacharyya, advised initial purchasers and represented the financing parties in their commitments. The firm also provided tax guidance through Bryant Lee and Lucas Migliano, and addressed environmental considerations with Joshua Marnitz and Nolan Fargo.

+ LUMEN EXPANDS CLOUD NETWORKING FOOTPRINT WITH ALKIRA BUYOUT VALUED AT $475 MILLION

Lumen Technologies is pursuing growth by acquiring Alkira for US$475 million in an all-cash transaction, aimed at broadening its connectivity across hybrid and multi-cloud deployments. The company expects the deal to close in the third quarter of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals and closing conditions. Lumen has engaged Latham & Watkins LLP to advise on the deal. The corporate deal team is led by partners Bradley Faris, based in Chicago and New York, with a broader roster handling compensation and benefits, labor and employment, intellectual property, data privacy, antitrust, tax and environmental matters, underscoring the firm's capability in large, complex transactions. Alkira provides a cloud-native, carrier-agnostic platform that helps enterprises design, deploy and manage connectivity across varied cloud environments. By adding Alkira, Lumen seeks to accelerate its cloud networking strategy and deliver more flexible, scalable network solutions to customers. The transaction reflects ongoing consolidation in the technology sector as firms migrate to cloud services and demand robust networking. Regulators will review the deal during closing, and Lumen plans to integrate Alkira's technologies into its existing infrastructure, expanding its footprint in the cloud networking market. Ultimately, the move aligns with Lumen's strategy to broaden its service offerings and capitalize on a growing segment of enterprise IT.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS AND LOEB & LOEB GUIDE AI BATTERY COMPANY THROUGH $250 MILLION SPAC MERGER

Latham & Watkins and Loeb & Loeb are at the forefront of a $250 million SPAC merger involving AI Battery Company, a move designed to expand access to public markets and accelerate growth in AI-powered battery technology.The deal was announced on 21st April 2026 and centers on AI Battery Company's work in advanced battery solutions that leverage artificial intelligence to improve efficiency and sustainability. The funding will support increased research and development and the expansion of manufacturing capabilities.Latham & Watkins brings deep experience in SPAC transactions and capital markets, while Loeb & Loeb adds regulatory compliance and corporate governance guidance. Investors have been closely watching the negotiations as demand for AI-enabled solutions continues to rise.The SPAC structure provides a clear path to a public listing and greater visibility within the investment community, with both firms underscoring a smooth transition as closing conditions are met.As the deal moves toward completion, counsel say the collaboration will play a key role in navigating regulatory review and ensuring a strong launch for AI Battery Company in the public markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [3 News That Matters]

+ DREW BARBER REJOINS SKADDEN TO STRENGTHEN GLOBAL M&A PRACTICE

Skadden announced the return of Drew Barber as a partner in its M&A practice in Boston, signaling a deliberate expansion of the firm’s deal-making capabilities. Barber most recently served as senior vice president and general counsel of a multinational aerospace and defense company, where he led strategic acquisitions and managed a team of about 1,000 professionals. The firm disclosed the move on 6th May 2026. Barber brings a broad corporate-law background, advising on mergers, acquisitions, divestitures, governance and securities law, with experience across aerospace and defense, manufacturing, technology, financial services and real estate. Allison Schneirov, global head of Skadden’s transactions practices, welcomed the return, saying, "He brings a powerful combination of strategic M&A experience, trusted counsel to executive leadership, and a genuine understanding of what matters most to in-house legal teams." Moshe Spinowitz, head of Skadden’s Boston office, highlighted the value of Barber's industry knowledge and in-house perspective. Barber himself expressed enthusiasm about rejoining Skadden, noting the firm’s global platform and depth of talent as assets for clients facing complex transactions and regulatory challenges, and adding, "After years of advising on high-stakes business and legal matters as a general counsel, I am excited to bring that firsthand perspective to our clients as they navigate increasingly complex transactions and regulatory challenges." The executive previously worked as an associate at Skadden, where he advised on M&A, capital markets and corporate governance. He earned a J.D. from the University of Connecticut School of Law and an undergraduate degree from Trinity College, underscoring Skadden’s ongoing commitment to delivering innovative legal solutions.

+ SKADDEN EXPANDS LONDON PARTNERSHIP IN 55-PARTNER GLOBAL ROUND

Skadden has promoted nine London partners as part of a 55-strong global round, the firm's first promotions since adding a non-equity partner tier. The shift aligns with a broader push among US firms to recalibrate pay and broaden flexibility in partnership structures.This year's group more than doubles last year's 22, and London's intake is up sharply from the City's historically modest rounds.Skadden has long taken a selective approach in London, and the expanded round marks a notable shift in that posture.The nine new London partners span White Collar Defense and Investigations, Finance, Corporate, Tax, National Security, Financial Institutions and Capital Markets. They are Jonathan Benson, Zoe Q. Cooper Sutton, Benjamin T. Davies, Kara L. Heggs, Jason Hewitt, Nicola Kerr-Shaw, Feargal Ryan, Kenneth J. Ryan and Cristina Tomassini.Earlier this year the firm made a high-profile lateral hire in London with disputes specialists Sophie Lamb KC and Samuel Papef from Latham; Skadden declined to comment at press time.

+ OTSUKA ADVANCES BIOTECH PORTFOLIO WITH TRANSCEND THERAPEUTICS PURCHASE, SKADDEN ADVISES

Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom is advising Tokyo-based Otsuka Pharmaceutical on its USD1.2 billion acquisition of clinical-stage biotechnology company Transcend Therapeutics. As of 1st April 2026, the deal positions Otsuka to enhance its presence in a competitive biotech landscape.The agreement provides for USD700 million to be paid to Transcend shareholders at closing, expected in the second quarter of 2026. Up to USD525 million in additional contingent consideration may be triggered based on future sales milestones tied to assets in development.Transcend’s counsel is led by Kirkland & Ellis, with partners Graham Robinson and Laura Knoll, supported by Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman partner Ron Fleming Jr. Centerview Partners serves as Transcend’s financial adviser, with Andrew S Rymer and Jason Zuckerbrod, along with principal Tommaso Verderame. The arrangement illustrates ongoing biotech M&A activity as firms seek to expand capabilities amid regulatory complexity and shifting market dynamics.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS [3 News That Matters]

+ TWIN BRIDGE CAPITAL RAISES $855 MILLION FOR SIXTH FUND, WITH KIRKLAND & ELLIS ADVISORY

As of 5th May 2026, Kirkland & Ellis has helped Twin Bridge Capital Partners close its sixth fund at $855 million, marking a milestone for the firm. The fundraising underscores robust investor confidence in Twin Bridge's lower middle-market strategy. Twin Bridge will deploy the capital to growth-oriented companies across sectors, expanding its portfolio. Kirkland & Ellis provided legal and strategic support, navigating complex financing structures and regulatory considerations. The close highlights resilience in the private equity landscape and could signal renewed fundraising activity as market conditions remain favorable.

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS ELEVATES HONG KONG DISPUTES GROUP WITH PARTNER APPOINTMENT

Kirkland & Ellis has named Wilfred Ho as a new partner in its Hong Kong litigation practice, part of a broader push to expand the firm's capabilities in the Asia-Pacific region. The firm announced the hire on 29th April 2026.Ho previously served as a partner at White & Case in Hong Kong, specializing in international arbitration and commercial litigation. He brings more than a decade of experience advising Asian corporates and private equity funds on complex disputes across Greater China, with an earlier tenure at Skadden shaping his commercial practice.His practice includes shareholder disputes, insolvency and restructuring, and intellectual property matters, with clients across gaming, media, entertainment, hospitality, technology and energy sectors.In arbitration, Ho has represented clients under HKIAC, CIETAC, ICC, NAI and SIAC rules.Neil McDonald, a Kirkland & Ellis partner, welcomed Ho as a transformative addition to the Asia litigation team, noting his cross-border experience and deep understanding of Greater China’s business landscape will help clients navigate high-stakes disputes.

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS EXPANDS LONDON PRIVATE CAPITAL CAPABILITIES WITH WILL AITKEN-DAVIES

Kirkland & Ellis has announced the addition of Will Aitken-Davies as a corporate partner in its London office, expanding its private capital practice. The hire is effective immediately and aims to strengthen capabilities in private equity and infrastructure investor transactions across European and cross-border markets. The announcement was made on 29th April 2026 at 16:34:10.Aitken-Davies arrives from Paul Weiss, bringing a track record in acquisitions, carve-outs, co-investments, public M&A, consortium deals, and hybrid equity investments. His sector coverage includes energy and infrastructure, industrials, technology, and financial services; he is ranked in Chambers UK for Private Equity Buyouts, as well as in Legal 500 UK and IFLR1000.Will is described as a trusted adviser to leading financial investors on sophisticated, cross-border European transactions, a quality that strengthens Kirkland’s capacity to support sponsors executing at scale across London and Europe, said Jon A. Ballis, Chairman of the Executive Committee. Matthew Elliott, Executive Committee Member, noted that Aitken-Davies’ European M&A experience will enhance the firm’s ability to provide integrated, top-tier advice.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 News That Matters]

+ TEXAS GROWTH FUELS SIMPSON THACHER'S TEN-YEAR TRANSFORMATION

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett is expanding its Texas footprint, posting a decade of growth that includes a 26 percent rise in headcount and a 34 percent jump in revenue in 2025. By 5th May 2026 the firm has positioned itself among the state's leading dealmakers, building on progress since missing the Texas Lawbook 50 and Corporate Deal Tracker in 2015. Houston's leadership stresses that growth starts with people, supported by strong momentum in energy and infrastructure, as well as AI and power demand. The Texas Lawbook 50 shows 82 lawyers in Texas, up from 66 the prior year and 21 in 2015, underscoring the expansion. Texas revenue rose to $187.8 million in 2025, from $25.6 million in 2015, and revenue per Texas lawyer reached $2.254 million, well ahead of the firm-wide figure. In the first quarter of 2026 the team has continued to win complex matters for clients, signaling sustained momentum. The Corporate Deal Tracker data show 14 Texas M&A deals exceeding $1 billion in 2025. Shamus Crosby led or co-led five billion-dollar transactions totaling $40.5 billion, while Breen Haire played a key role in three billion-dollar transactions totaling $30 billion, including the $22.2 billion Sempra Infrastructure Partners deal by KKR. Looking ahead, Simpson Thacher plans a second Dallas office later this year to deepen its Texas footprint, a move aimed at serving its private equity and banking clients and reinforcing the firm’s position in Texas dealmaking.

+ KKR HIRES SIMPSON THACHER FOR TAIYO HOLDINGS TAKE-PRIVATE AS STAKEHOLDERS ALIGN

KKR has unveiled plans to acquire all outstanding shares of Taiyo Holdings for 528.56 billion yen (about $3.3 billion) via a tender offer, with Simpson Thacher & Bartlett serving as adviser. The announcement was made on 10th April 2026.The bid sets the offer at 4,750 yen per share, a 4.7% discount to Taiyo's last close. Major shareholders including DIC Corp, Kowa Co and Oasis Management hold a combined 42.2% of Taiyo’s stock, providing substantial backing for the privatization.Simpson Thacher has assembled a dedicated team to assist KKR, including partners Noritaka Kumamoto and Jonathan Stradling, with Daisuke Ueta and Kaoruko Sakuramoto focusing on mergers and acquisitions. Antitrust work is led by Etienne Renaudeau with Dani Rabinowitz, Luke Cowdell and Giovanna De Cristofaro; credit-related matters are overseen by Makiko Harunari, Atsushi Usui and Mark Watson.

+ NSSK IV SERIES FUNDS CLOSE AT JPY250 BILLION WITH SIMPSON THACHER COUNSEL

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett advised the Japanese private equity firm Nippon Sangyo Suishin Kiko (NSSK) on forming the NSSK IV Series Funds, securing total commitments of JPY250 billion (USD1.5 billion). The milestone was marked on 10th April 2026, with the fund offering closing on 31st March 2026.A global team at Simpson Thacher provided comprehensive counsel, emphasizing regulatory and compliance matters critical to the fund's formation. The engagement featured David Azcue, co-head of the private funds practice in Japan; Adam Furber, co-head of the Asia offices and head of the Asia investment funds practice; and Makiko Harunari, head of the Asia banking and credit practice.The NSSK IV Series Funds were more than twice oversubscribed, drawing commitments from over 100 institutional investors worldwide. The closing underscores robust investor confidence in NSSK's strategy and signals growing interest in private equity in the Asia-Pacific region.As M&A activity rises across Asia, the role of legal counsel continues to evolve, with firms like Simpson Thacher adapting to regulatory complexities and shifting market dynamics. The firm’s guidance facilitated the fund's launch and demonstrates its ability to address private equity challenges in the region.With the NSSK IV Series Funds in place, NSSK is positioned to pursue new investment opportunities and contribute to Japan's private equity landscape, illustrating the synergy between legal expertise and investment strategy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WACHTELL, LIPTON, ROSEN & KATZ LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SYSCO NAMES LEADING FIRMS TO OVERSEE $29 BILLION JETRO RESTAURANT DEPOT MERGER

Sysco Corporation has engaged Jones Day for antitrust counsel as it moves to acquire rival Jetro Restaurant Depot in a deal valued at $29 billion. The law firm Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP has also been engaged to advise on the merger, with the arrangement confirmed on 31st March 2026.The deal aims to strengthen Sysco’s position in the North American food-distribution market, where margins are squeezed by costs and supply-chain disruption. Jetro’s extensive network in the restaurant-supply sector would complement Sysco’s footprint and allow broader offerings for customers, though the size of the transaction signals a comprehensive regulatory review.Jones Day, known for its robust antitrust practice, will guide Sysco through regulatory scrutiny as competition authorities assess the merger. Wachtell, recognized for handling complex mergers, is expected to play a critical role in shaping the legal strategy and response to potential objections.As the largest food distributor in North America, Sysco’s expansion through this acquisition reflects a broader industry trend toward consolidation to improve efficiency and service. The outcome could alter competitive dynamics and set a precedent for future deals in the sector.Investors will closely watch the process, given the potential implications for Sysco, Jetro and the wider food-distribution market. The joint effort by Jones Day and Wachtell emphasizes the importance of experienced counsel in navigating high-profile mergers under heightened regulatory scrutiny.

+ PUBLIC STORAGE TO ACQUIRE NATIONAL STORAGE AFFILIATES FOR $10.5 BILLION IN ALL-STOCK DEAL; CLIFFORD CHANCE AND WACHTELL LIPTON LEAD COUNSEL

On 24th March 2026, Public Storage announced its plan to acquire National Storage Affiliates in a $10.5 billion all-stock transaction, a move that would further elevate its status as the largest self-storage company in the United States by market capitalization. Wachtell Lipton is advising Public Storage, led by Adam Emmerich, co-chair of the corporate department, and Kyle Diamond, with Clifford Chance representing National Storage Affiliates; the Clifford Chance team is led by M&A partners Chang-Do Gong and Robert Chung, who recently joined from White & Case, with Andrew Epstein and Jeanne Roig-Irwin also on the matter.Under the terms, NSA shareholders will receive 0.14 Public Storage common shares for each NSA share, valuing the consideration at $41.68 per share. The closing is expected in the third quarter of 2026 after the formation of a joint venture that will encompass 313 properties with an estimated value of about $3.3 billion. Public Storage will rely on DLA Piper as real estate financing counsel, led by Amy Carbins with Viktor Sapezhnikov supporting.The combined company would have a market capitalization near $57 billion, reflecting its expanded footprint in key U.S. markets.

+ ELON MUSK'S X CORP TERMINATES $90M LITIGATION WITH WACHTELL LIPTON

Elon Musk’s X Corp has abruptly exited a high-stakes $90 million lawsuit against Wachtell Lipton Rosen & Katz, terminating a contentious chapter stemming from the 2022 Twitter takeover. The San Francisco Superior Court filing dated October 1, 2023, accused the firm of securing an 'improper bonus payment' that breached fiduciary duties, alleging that Twitter executives boosted fees with 'success' bonuses just before the $44 billion deal closed.Wachtell Lipton, which had represented the old Twitter board in the bid to force Musk to honor his buyout pledge, pushed back vigorously. The firm highlighted its 'complete success' after months of litigation, saying it secured billions for shareholders and that the fees in question were approved by the board itself, countering X’s claims.Reuters reported the court filing revealing the dismissal, which blocks refiling and signals a clean separation between the parties. A Wachtell Lipton spokesman said, 'We are pleased that X has dismissed its meritless lawsuit with prejudice. There was no settlement.' This development marks a major turning point in the Twitter acquisition saga.Following a separate development, X Corp had recently acknowledged a $128 million executive payout suit tied to the tumult of the acquisition period. The dismissal may reflect a strategic shift for X Corp as it seeks to disentangle itself from the financial and legal headaches of its predecessor.As the legal landscape shifts for Elon Musk’s X Corp, the resolution with Wachtell Lipton could enable a more concentrated focus on operation, with investors watching closely how the move shapes the company’s path ahead.

+ CRAVATH, SWAINE & MOORE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE TO DRIVE £800 MILLION CAMPAIGN FOR WATER INFRASTRUCTURE

On 30th of April 2026, Cravath Swaine & Moore said it would lead a £800 million fundraising campaign intended to bolster water sustainability initiatives, drawing interest from institutional and private investors seeking exposure to sustainable water management.The plan targets technologies and infrastructure upgrades that improve water efficiency and reduce waste, with Cravath advising on the financial architecture and deal structuring and Slaughter and May providing support on regulatory compliance and corporate transactions.The organizers expect this effort to not only fill capital needs but also set a benchmark for future water projects, signaling investor confidence in a growing market as environmental considerations rise globally.The drive could shift investment patterns in the water sector, as partnerships with key stakeholders are pursued to accelerate deployment.

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE VETERAN FORMS NICHE CORPORATE INVESTIGATIONS FIRM

A partner from Cravath Swaine & Moore has announced the creation of a boutique law firm focused exclusively on corporate investigations, reflecting growing demand for specialized counsel amid tightening regulatory environments. The practice will concentrate on internal investigations, compliance assessments, and risk-management strategies tailored to each client.On 9th March 2026, the launch highlighted a broader industry shift toward leaner, more agile structures as senior practitioners pursue targeted specialties. The boutique intends to leverage the founder’s Cravath background to deliver high-caliber work with a more personalized touch.Industry insiders expect the client base to include Fortune 500 companies, private equity firms, and startups seeking discreet, experienced handling of sensitive investigations. The founder’s established network and Cravath lineage are seen as key assets in building credibility and market presence.

+ BIOCON COMPLETES $1.2 BILLION BIOSIMILARS PUSH, CONSOLIDATING STAKEHOLDINGS FROM MINORITY PARTNERS

Biocon Ltd, the Indian biopharmaceutical company, disclosed on December 11, 2025 a $1.2 billion deal to consolidate its biosimilars business by purchasing the remaining stakes held by minority partners. The move follows Biocon's strategy to simplify governance, accelerate integration with Viatris, and refinance debt ahead of potential market headwinds in biosimilars.Under the agreement, Viatris will sell its remaining stake for about $815 million. Other sellers include True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II. Serum Institute Life Sciences was also part of the stake sale, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas advising on the matter. On financing aspects, Khaitan & Co and Cravath, Swaine & Moore advised Viatris, while Veritas Legal supported True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II.Biocon's advisory was led by Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas, with partners Raghubir Menon and Natashaa Shroff at the helm, and a broader team that included Tarumoy Chaudhuri and Kriti Saraswat. The transaction illustrates a broad, multi-jurisdictional legal framework, reflecting the involvement of global and domestic law firms across different segments of the deal.Analysts say the consolidation is expected to sharpen Biocon's competitive edge in biosimilars and help push forward the Viatris integration and debt refinancing. Reuters has noted the deal's complexity, underscoring the cross-border negotiations and number of stakeholders involved.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK LAUNCHES 2026-2027 COHORTS FOR FELLOWS AND EMERGING LEADERS PROGRAMS

On the 1st of May, 2026, the Milbank Memorial Fund opened applications for the 2026-2027 cohorts of its Milbank Fellows Program (MFP) and Emerging Leaders Program (ELP), programs designed to strengthen health policy leadership in government. The applications will be accepted through June 19th, 2026. The MFP is aimed at senior leaders in state governments, while the ELP targets rising leaders in state, large-county, and large-city jurisdictions. Participants will have all associated costs covered and will have opportunities to collaborate with peers from different regions. The curricula focus on leadership development, mastery of complex health policy topics, and the ability to translate policy knowledge into practical improvements in communities. Milbank seeks diverse cohorts reflecting a broad spectrum of political and geographic backgrounds. Selection criteria emphasize a commitment to health improvement and addressing health disparities; candidates should hold influential roles in the legislative or executive branches and be prepared to learn and share expertise on population health policy. The Fund invites government officials to apply or nominate candidates who meet these qualifications. For more details and application guidelines, interested parties can visit the Milbank website program pages.

+ MILBANK SOLICITOR TRAINEE SETS GUINNESS RECORD FOR FASTEST COSTUME MARATHON

Archie Hunt, a trainee solicitor at Milbank, set a Guinness World Record by finishing the London Marathon in a Captain Underpants costume in 2 hours 27 minutes on Apr. 28, 2026. His time eclipsed the previous mark of 2:42 for the fastest marathon dressed as a book character.Balancing a demanding legal role with months of rigorous training, Hunt clocked early-morning sessions of more than 20 kilometers while in the full Captain Underpants outfit. The record attempt followed strict Guinness World Records guidelines, with official timing data, witness statements, and photographic evidence to validate the feat.The run carried personal significance, as Hunt was fundraising for Evelina London Children’s Charity, connected to the hospital where he is treated for dilated cardiomyopathy. Despite the condition potentially adding 10 to 15 minutes, Hunt finished in the top 0.1% of participants at a World Major marathon.In a statement after crossing the line, Hunt said, "It feels incredible to have broken the costume record and to now be the fastest recorded marathoner diagnosed with dilated cardiomyopathy." The achievement has attracted sponsorship interest and podcast invitations, and he is eyeing future races, including a 110km ultra in Finland scheduled for June, with a goal of a sub-2:20 time.

+ MILBANK BETS ON 'HERD IMMUNITY' TO FIGHT EMERALD ASH BORER

Milbank has unveiled a comprehensive plan to shield its ash trees from the emerald ash borer, a woodboring beetle that has caused widespread damage across parts of the United States. The city notes that ash trees account for more than 50% of its canopy, including trees in parks and the cemetery, raising the stakes for an aggressive response.On Apr. 21, 2026, city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial picture: removing affected trees could cost as much as $4 million. To counter the threat, Milbank is pursuing a 'herd immunity' approach that would run for about 12 years at an estimated $2.4 million, focusing on treatments and monitoring to bolster tree health rather than wholesale removal.Proponents argue that protecting the ash canopy supports benefits such as cleaner air, better water retention, and wildlife habitat. The plan envisions a phased program, starting with the most vulnerable areas, and relies on collaboration among officials, scientists, and residents.If successful, Milbank’s strategy could serve as a template for other communities facing similar challenges and demonstrate how proactive stewardship can preserve urban forests amid invasive pressures.

urn:contentItem:6JJV-8W83-RXDC-D1CP-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:27ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREA INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S NEW ETF CAPTIVATES INVESTORS WITH 100 BILLION WON MILESTONE

The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF, managed by Korea Investment Management Co., Ltd., has captivated the market by surpassing 100 billion won in net assets in less than a week since its listing. As of 22nd April 2026, the net asset value of the ETF, which debuted on the Korea Exchange on 14th April 2026, reached 103.7 billion won, representing a staggering ninefold increase from its initial 11 billion won on the first trading day.Unlike traditional ETFs that focus solely on aerospace and defense sectors, Korea Investment Management's active ETF is strategically concentrated on 'NewSpace' services. This includes companies engaged in reusable launch vehicles, artificial intelligence-based satellite data analytics, and space data centers. Notable holdings include EchoStar (22.65%), Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, and MDA Space. EchoStar, which has a stake in SpaceX, holds the highest weighting among all ETFs listed in Korea, while MDA Space and York Space Systems are exclusive to this ETF.The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF has demonstrated impressive performance since its launch, delivering a return of 7.63%, the highest among the nine space and aerospace ETFs currently available in Korea. This strong performance has attracted significant interest from individual investors, who have cumulatively net purchased 52.6 billion won over the six trading days following the ETF's introduction.Kim Hyuntae, Head of Global Quantitative Investment at Korea Investment Management and the fund's portfolio manager, emphasized the importance of an agile approach to managing the ETF. He stated, 'Given that the space industry is still in the early stages of growth, we determined that it is important to construct a portfolio focused on high-growth, pure space technology companies and to respond quickly to market changes, so we launched the product as an active ETF.' He further noted that the active management allows for swift incorporation of new listings, such as SpaceX, directly responding to market events.As the space industry continues to evolve, Korea Investment Management's ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The rapid accumulation of assets within a short timeframe underscores the growing investor confidence in this innovative approach to space technology investments.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION SPOTLIGHTS MID-MARKET OPPORTUNITIES AT NEW YORK FORUM

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) hosted the 28th 'New York Finance Forum' at the Korea Society in New York, focusing on the burgeoning mid-market segment in North American infrastructure investments. The event took place on the 16th of April 2026, drawing over 40 participants from government, public investment institutions, and various financial sectors.During the forum, Northleaf Capital Partners presented key insights into the mid-market infrastructure investment landscape, highlighting its structural advantages. This segment typically includes deals involving companies valued at approximately USD 1 billion or less, though the threshold has recently expanded to include transactions up to USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics and asset manager criteria.Jared Waldron, Co-Head of Infrastructure at Northleaf, underscored that despite 86% of North American infrastructure investment transactions occurring within the mid-market, institutional funds remain heavily concentrated in larger funds. This disparity creates a less competitive environment, allowing investors to secure more favorable investment opportunities.Jessica Kennedy, Managing Director at Northleaf, further elaborated on the advantages of mid-market infrastructure investments, noting that they offer opportunities for differentiated results through active value creation and multiple exit strategies. Investors can thus anticipate attractive risk-adjusted returns from this segment.Yuryoung Kim, Head of KIC's New York office and organizer of the forum, expressed optimism that the discussions would assist domestic institutional investors in refining their overseas alternative investment strategies. KIC continues to operate in major global financial hubs, including New York, San Francisco, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, facilitating the exchange of investment information and fostering collaborative networks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-3NN3-RSP0-R4DF-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:27ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREA INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S NEW ETF CAPTIVATES INVESTORS WITH 100 BILLION WON MILESTONE

The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF, managed by Korea Investment Management Co., Ltd., has captivated the market by surpassing 100 billion won in net assets in less than a week since its listing. As of 22nd April 2026, the net asset value of the ETF, which debuted on the Korea Exchange on 14th April 2026, reached 103.7 billion won, representing a staggering ninefold increase from its initial 11 billion won on the first trading day.Unlike traditional ETFs that focus solely on aerospace and defense sectors, Korea Investment Management's active ETF is strategically concentrated on 'NewSpace' services. This includes companies engaged in reusable launch vehicles, artificial intelligence-based satellite data analytics, and space data centers. Notable holdings include EchoStar (22.65%), Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, and MDA Space. EchoStar, which has a stake in SpaceX, holds the highest weighting among all ETFs listed in Korea, while MDA Space and York Space Systems are exclusive to this ETF.The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF has demonstrated impressive performance since its launch, delivering a return of 7.63%, the highest among the nine space and aerospace ETFs currently available in Korea. This strong performance has attracted significant interest from individual investors, who have cumulatively net purchased 52.6 billion won over the six trading days following the ETF's introduction.Kim Hyuntae, Head of Global Quantitative Investment at Korea Investment Management and the fund's portfolio manager, emphasized the importance of an agile approach to managing the ETF. He stated, 'Given that the space industry is still in the early stages of growth, we determined that it is important to construct a portfolio focused on high-growth, pure space technology companies and to respond quickly to market changes, so we launched the product as an active ETF.' He further noted that the active management allows for swift incorporation of new listings, such as SpaceX, directly responding to market events.As the space industry continues to evolve, Korea Investment Management's ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The rapid accumulation of assets within a short timeframe underscores the growing investor confidence in this innovative approach to space technology investments.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION SPOTLIGHTS MID-MARKET OPPORTUNITIES AT NEW YORK FORUM

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) hosted the 28th 'New York Finance Forum' at the Korea Society in New York, focusing on the burgeoning mid-market segment in North American infrastructure investments. The event took place on the 16th of April 2026, drawing over 40 participants from government, public investment institutions, and various financial sectors.During the forum, Northleaf Capital Partners presented key insights into the mid-market infrastructure investment landscape, highlighting its structural advantages. This segment typically includes deals involving companies valued at approximately USD 1 billion or less, though the threshold has recently expanded to include transactions up to USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics and asset manager criteria.Jared Waldron, Co-Head of Infrastructure at Northleaf, underscored that despite 86% of North American infrastructure investment transactions occurring within the mid-market, institutional funds remain heavily concentrated in larger funds. This disparity creates a less competitive environment, allowing investors to secure more favorable investment opportunities.Jessica Kennedy, Managing Director at Northleaf, further elaborated on the advantages of mid-market infrastructure investments, noting that they offer opportunities for differentiated results through active value creation and multiple exit strategies. Investors can thus anticipate attractive risk-adjusted returns from this segment.Yuryoung Kim, Head of KIC's New York office and organizer of the forum, expressed optimism that the discussions would assist domestic institutional investors in refining their overseas alternative investment strategies. KIC continues to operate in major global financial hubs, including New York, San Francisco, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, facilitating the exchange of investment information and fostering collaborative networks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.
